title: NEW ROADS SCHOOL - 211info
text: NEW ROADS NEW ROADS SCHOOL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational services for homeless, runaway, and at-risk of becoming homeless youth. Classes for credit include middle and high school basics of language arts, math, sciences, and social studies. Students will also be offered GED preparation/tutoring. Students will receive support with resumes, employment applications, forms, and bills. In some situations, class times can be arranged to fit individual needs. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless, runaway, and at-risk of becoming homeless youth ages 11-21 Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm Email (541) 302-2551 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 302-2551 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Youth may self refer and can call or walk in to the school. Referrals can also be made by social service agencies, businesses, neighbors, and religious organizations. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: 7 days per week 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: On LTD bus lines 51 and 53 Services: Alternative Education GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION HOUSING/SHELTER - LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS HOUSING/SHELTER - NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. NEW ROADS
